title: Aleuritopteris chrysophylla
taxonomy: Aleuritopteris chrysophylla is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Aleuritopteris and the order Pteridales. This classification places Aleuritopteris chrysophylla within the class Polypodiopsida and the division Magnoliophyta. The kingdom to which it belongs is Plantae. The specific classification of Aleuritopteris chrysophylla may vary depending on the source used.
uses: Aleuritopteris chrysophylla has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their possible applications in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Aleuritopteris chrysophylla for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Aleuritopteris chrysophylla include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and flu.
